rules.rule: Pin testing tooling to the declared versions (pytest 8.3.x, pytest-asyncio 0.24.x, pytest-cov 5.0.x, httpx 0.27.x, Python 3.12; vitest 2.1.x, @testing-library/react 16.x, @playwright/test 1.48.x, Node 20 LTS) and do not upgrade ad hoc.
rules.rationale: Tech KB declares explicit version pins so async, React 19, and Playwright browser binaries stay mutually compatible; drift causes subtle fixture or JSX breakage.

rules.rule: Set asyncio_mode = "auto" in pyproject.toml [tool.pytest.ini_options] and use @pytest_asyncio.fixture (not @pytest.fixture) for any async fixture.
rules.rationale: asyncio_mode=auto removes the need to mark every async test, but fixtures still require the asyncio-aware decorator; mixing them silently skips setup.

rules.rule: Always configure pytest with addopts including -ra --strict-markers --strict-config and --cov=backend --cov-report=term-missing, and register every custom marker in pyproject.toml.
rules.rationale: --strict-markers prevents typo markers from silently being ignored; --strict-config surfaces misconfiguration; coverage output on every run keeps gaps visible.

rules.rule: Use @pytest.mark.parametrize to cover boundary cases explicitly (e.g., Kelly fraction at p=0.5, p<0.5 clamped to 0, p>0.5 positive) and assign readable ids via pytest.param(..., id="...").
rules.rationale: Parametrize with named ids surfaces exactly which boundary failed; the KB's kelly_fraction and sharpe examples show boundary + alternating cases are mandatory.

rules.rule: Compare floats with pytest.approx(..., rel=..., abs=...), never with ==.
rules.rationale: Direct float equality is listed as a gotcha that "will burn you"; approx pins tolerance explicitly.

rules.rule: Keep DB and HTTP fixtures function-scoped by default; only promote to session scope (like the in-memory async engine) when state must persist across the whole run, and always teardown with yield.
rules.rationale: Function-scope is the pytest default and prevents cross-test state leakage; session scope is reserved for expensive objects like the SQLAlchemy async engine.

rules.rule: Integration tests against FastAPI must use httpx.AsyncClient with ASGITransport(app=app) and base_url="http://test" inside an async context manager, not a live network server.
rules.rationale: ASGITransport runs the app in-process for deterministic, fast integration tests without binding a port; the KB uses this pattern for /api/markets.

rules.rule: Mock outbound adapter HTTP calls (Kalshi, Polymarket, etc.) with respx_mock inside async tests rather than hitting real APIs.
rules.rationale: respx mocks httpx at the transport layer so adapter unit/integration tests stay deterministic and offline-safe; the KB shows the polymarket fetch_market example.

rules.rule: Provide factory fixtures (e.g., make_market) that accept **overrides over sensible defaults; do not hand-construct model objects in each test.
rules.rationale: Factories centralize defaults (id, platform, prices, closes_at) so tests stay focused on the variable under test and resist schema churn.

rules.rule: Freeze time with @freeze_time (freezegun / pytest-freezegun) for any code that reads the current time, and seed randomness to prevent flaky runs.
rules.rationale: The KB's news_spike_cutoff test demonstrates deterministic time; freezing time and seeding RNG are the documented flakiness-prevention mechanisms.

rules.rule: Use monkeypatch.setenv for env var mutation inside tests; never mutate os.environ directly.
rules.rationale: monkeypatch auto-reverts at test teardown; direct os.environ edits leak state across tests.

rules.rule: Tag slow or external-dependency tests with @pytest.mark.integration or @pytest.mark.slow so the default fast run is `pytest -m "not integration and not slow"`.
rules.rationale: Marker-driven selection keeps CI fast tiers clean; the KB registers unit/integration/slow markers for exactly this purpose.

rules.rule: Enforce a minimum coverage gate of --cov-fail-under=80 in CI, and ensure critical paths (paper-fill insertion, strategy decide loop, metric computation, backtest replay) each have dedicated tests.
rules.rationale: The KB documents `pytest --cov=backend --cov-fail-under=80` as the canonical gate; these domains are the load-bearing logic of the system.

rules.rule: Place shared fixtures in conftest.py at the appropriate directory level; remember the closest conftest.py wins and can shadow higher-scope fixtures.
rules.rationale: The KB calls out conftest shadowing as a gotcha; placing fixtures thoughtfully avoids accidental overrides.

rules.rule: Configure Vitest with environment="jsdom", globals=true, setupFiles=["./vitest.setup.ts"], include=["**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}"], exclude=["**/node_modules/**", "**/e2e/**"], and coverage thresholds lines: 70.
rules.rationale: Excluding e2e keeps Playwright specs out of the Vitest runner; the KB's config is the documented baseline.

rules.rule: In vitest.setup.ts import "@testing-library/jest-dom/vitest" (not the non-vitest entry) and register afterEach(cleanup) from @testing-library/react.
rules.rationale: Wrong jest-dom entry silently omits matchers; missing cleanup leaks DOM between component tests.

rules.rule: Write React component tests with @testing-library/react using role-based queries (getByRole, getByLabel) and userEvent for interactions; assert visible text/state, not implementation details.
rules.rationale: The MarketCard and PlatformSelect examples query by role and visible text, which mirrors real user behavior and survives refactors.

rules.rule: Do not attempt to render React Server Components through Testing Library; extract logic into pure functions or Client Components for unit testing.
rules.rationale: RSCs are not real React components at runtime and cannot be rendered by RTL; the KB lists this as a gotcha.

rules.rule: Mock fetch in component/unit tests with vi.stubGlobal("fetch", ...); when using vi.mock(...), remember it is hoisted and use vi.hoisted() if the factory must reference outer variables.
rules.rationale: vi.mock hoisting is a documented footgun; stubGlobal is the KB's recommended pattern for fetch.

rules.rule: For time-dependent component logic (debounce, polling) use vi.useFakeTimers() + vi.advanceTimersByTime(...) and always restore with vi.useRealTimers() at the end.
rules.rationale: Fake timers are the deterministic way to test debounced UI; forgetting to restore leaks timers across tests.

rules.rule: Run Playwright E2E with a dual webServer array starting both Next.js (`pnpm dev` on 3000) and FastAPI (`uv run uvicorn backend.app.main:app --port 8000`), each with reuseExistingServer: !process.env.CI and timeout 120_000.
rules.rationale: E2E tests will hit a dead backend if only Next is started; the dual webServer pattern is the KB's prescribed setup.

rules.rule: Playwright config must set baseURL="http://localhost:3000", retries: process.env.CI ? 2 : 0, trace: "on-first-retry", screenshot: "only-on-failure", and declare projects for chromium and firefox.
rules.rationale: These settings give CI retry-on-flake with trace artifacts for debugging while keeping local runs fast and deterministic.

rules.rule: Never call page.waitForTimeout or arbitrary sleeps in Playwright; rely on built-in auto-waiting via waitForSelector and expect(...).toBeVisible/toHaveURL/toContainText.
rules.rationale: The KB explicitly flags waitForTimeout as flaky; auto-waiting assertions are deterministic.

rules.rule: Seed backend state in E2E via page.request or direct API calls, not page.evaluate, and mock backend failures with page.route("**/api/...", route => route.fulfill({ status, body })) to exercise error UI paths.
rules.rationale: page.evaluate for setup is brittle; route.fulfill is the KB-recommended way to simulate 502s and verify error surfaces.

rules.rule: Name tests descriptively in given-when-then style (e.g., "filter by kalshi", "handles backend 502", "test_list_markets_empty") so failure output reads as a sentence.
rules.rationale: The KB's examples consistently use descriptive, behavior-oriented names; this keeps CI logs interpretable without opening the code.
